touches.ai_summary: Brief cold call with Kwame Nelson, new Head of RevOps at EastgateData. Kwame expressed interest in improving forecast accuracy and agreed to a follow-up discussion.

questions_asked.question: How are you currently managing your revenue forecasting?
questions_asked.answer: We're using spreadsheets and some basic Salesforce reports. It's pretty manual and I'm not confident in the accuracy.

next_steps: Send overview email with calendar link for qualification call

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sent with product overview and calendar link. Kwame responded confirming interest and scheduled qualification call.
next_steps: Qualification call scheduled for next week

touches.ai_summary: Qualification call with Kwame to understand current forecasting challenges at EastgateData. Discussed pain points with manual processes, lack of visibility, and need for better pipeline health insights. Kwame mentioned they're also looking at Clari.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the need to improve forecasting right now?
questions_asked.answer: I just took over RevOps three months ago and discovered our forecast accuracy is only around 60%. Leadership needs better visibility into pipeline health.

questions_asked.question: Who else is involved in evaluating forecasting tools?
questions_asked.answer: Right now it's mainly me. I'll need to get procurement involved eventually, and probably loop in some AMs to test it out.

questions_asked.question: How does your platform integrate with Salesforce?
questions_asked.answer: We have native bi-directional sync with Salesforce. Setup typically takes less than a day.

objections: We're also looking at Clari. How do you compare?
next_steps: Send competitive comparison materials
Schedule discovery call to dive deeper into use cases

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call focused on EastgateData's current tech stack, team structure, and specific forecasting needs. Kwame shared that they have 22 account managers and want automations plus some consulting help with implementation. Discussed timeline and budget considerations.

questions_asked.question: What does success look like for you in 6 months?
questions_asked.answer: Forecast accuracy above 85%, and our AMs spending less time on manual data entry and more time selling.

questions_asked.question: Do you offer implementation support or is it self-service?
questions_asked.answer: We have both options. Given your team size and goals, I'd recommend our automations plus consulting package.

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline for making a decision?
questions_asked.answer: Ideally we'd have something in place by end of quarter. Budget is approved for this fiscal year.

next_steps: Schedule demo with Kwame and 1-2 account managers
Prepare custom demo based on forecasting accuracy use case

touches.ai_summary: Full product demo with Kwame and two account managers. Walked through forecasting dashboard, pipeline health scoring, and automation capabilities. Team engaged with good questions about data requirements and rollout process. Kwame asked about pricing ranges.

questions_asked.question: What data do you need from our Salesforce to make the forecasting accurate?
questions_asked.answer: We pull standard opportunity fields, activity data, and historical close rates. The more historical data the better for the initial model training.

questions_asked.question: How long does it take to see value after implementation?
questions_asked.answer: Most customers see improved forecast accuracy within 30-45 days as the model learns from your data.

questions_asked.question: What does pricing typically look like for a team our size?
questions_asked.answer: For 22 seats with the automations plus consulting package, we're typically in the $18-25K ACV range depending on the specific configuration.

objections: One of the AMs mentioned Clari has more brand recognition in the market
next_steps: Share customer case studies from similar-sized companies
Follow up on additional questions from the AM team

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up call with Kwame to address questions from the demo and discuss next steps. Kwame shared that the AMs liked the platform but leadership is asking for more details on ROI and comparison with Clari.

questions_asked.question: What specific concerns does leadership have?
questions_asked.answer: They want to make sure we're picking the right tool. Clari is the more well-known option, so I need to build a strong business case.

questions_asked.question: Would it be helpful to have an executive briefing with our leadership team?
questions_asked.answer: Maybe later in the process. Right now I need to get Wyatt from procurement involved and build out the business case internally first.

next_steps: Send ROI calculator and competitive comparison
Schedule second demo focused on consulting services and implementation

touches.ai_summary: Email follow-up with ROI materials and competitive analysis. Kwame responded asking to reschedule the next demo due to conflicting priorities.
next_steps: Reschedule demo for the following week

touches.ai_summary: Rescheduled demo focused on implementation approach and consulting services. Kwame brought Wyatt Patel from procurement. Discussion covered contract terms, onboarding timeline, and support options. Wyatt asked detailed questions about vendor requirements and SLAs.

questions_asked.question: What's your standard contract length and payment terms?
questions_asked.answer: We typically do annual contracts with quarterly or annual payment options. We're flexible on payment terms.

questions_asked.question: What are your uptime guarantees and support SLAs?
questions_asked.answer: 99.9% uptime SLA with 24/7 support for critical issues. Business hours support for standard questions.

questions_asked.question: Are there any specific procurement requirements or approval processes we should be aware of?
questions_asked.answer: We'll need security review, standard vendor agreements, and CFO approval for anything over $15K.

next_steps: Send security documentation and standard contract
Schedule proposal review call
touches.reschedule_reason: prospect asked to push by a week

touches.ai_summary: Proposal review call with Kwame. Presented formal proposal at $23,100 ACV for 22 seats with automations plus consulting package. Kwame indicated the pricing is higher than expected and asked about flexibility given they're comparing with Clari's pricing.

questions_asked.question: Is there any flexibility on pricing given our team size and use case?
questions_asked.answer: This is still above our target budget. Clari came in lower. Can you work with us on price?

objections: Clari's proposal came in at a lower price point
next_steps: Discuss pricing options with sales leadership
Schedule call to review revised proposal

touches.ai_summary: Brief executive meeting attempted with Kwame to discuss strategic value and executive sponsorship. Call was short as Kwame mentioned he's still working on getting exec sponsor engaged and needs to focus on pricing first.

questions_asked.question: Would it make sense to get your VP of Sales or CRO involved in the conversation?
questions_asked.answer: Eventually, yes. But right now I need to come to them with a solid recommendation and pricing that works. Let's nail that down first.

next_steps: Continue pricing discussions
Plan for executive engagement once pricing is aligned

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation call rescheduled due to Kwame needing more time for internal budget discussions. When we connected, presented revised proposal at $18,250 ACV. Kwame appreciated the movement but indicated they need to get closer to $15K to make it work within their approved budget.

questions_asked.question: Our approved budget is $15K. Can you get to that number?
questions_asked.answer: We've already come down significantly. Let me discuss with leadership what's possible.

questions_asked.question: If we can find a way to make the numbers work, what's the approval process from there?
questions_asked.answer: I'd need to get Wyatt's sign-off, run it through security review, and then CFO approval. Probably 1-2 weeks if everything goes smoothly.

objections: Need to stay within $15K budget constraint
next_steps: Escalate pricing discussion internally
Schedule final negotiation call

touches.ai_summary: Executive meeting rescheduled twice before connecting. Finally held longer discussion with Kwame about creative options to bridge the gap. Discussed potential to reduce consulting hours, adjust payment terms, or phase implementation. Kwame mentioned internal pressure to make a decision soon but also concerns about committing without stronger exec buy-in.

questions_asked.question: Would it help to structure this as a smaller initial engagement with option to expand?
questions_asked.answer: Possibly, but I'm worried about piecemeal approach. I need something comprehensive enough to show real impact.

questions_asked.question: What if we reduced the consulting hours from 40 to 20 and focused on core implementation?
questions_asked.answer: That could help with budget, but I want to make sure we still have enough support for successful rollout.

questions_asked.question: Is there an executive who would champion this internally if we can align on terms?
questions_asked.answer: Our CRO cares about forecast accuracy, but honestly I haven't briefed her in detail yet. I wanted to have everything buttoned up first.

objections: Concern about lack of executive sponsor before committing
next_steps: Present final pricing proposal with reduced consulting
Discuss timeline for executive engagement
touches.reschedule_reason: prospect asked to push by a week

touches.ai_summary: Final pricing call with Kwame. Presented final offer at $14,600 ACV with 20 consulting hours instead of 40. Kwame indicated this works within budget and he'll move forward with internal approvals. Discussed implementation timeline and next steps for contract review. Still no executive sponsor directly engaged, but Kwame committed to briefing CRO this week.

questions_asked.question: This pricing works. What's the timeline to get contracts over for review?
questions_asked.answer: We can have contracts to you within 24 hours. How long does your security and legal review typically take?

questions_asked.question: When will you be able to get the CRO briefed and on board?
questions_asked.answer: I'll brief her this week. With the pricing sorted, I'm confident she'll support this.

next_steps: Send final contract for review
Kwame to brief CRO and initiate procurement process
Target signature within 2 weeks
